name: Lauren Lalonde, PramilaForCongress.com
subject: Reaching out about your supporter record
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
Going through our files and this is what we found for you:
#
Peter,
I can hardly believe it, but in just a few weeks, we’ll be at the end of the year -- and we have a long way to go if we want to reach the goal we set at the beginning of 2022.
It’s my responsibility to ensure our campaign and finances are where they need to be year-round, which is why I’m coming directly to you. Fundraising this year has slowed down dramatically compared to our last election year, which means we need to start building now to be better prepared next year.
We have 29 days left to make up our losses from this year, get back on track, and start 2023 in a strong position.
**We still have a lot of ground to cover through the rest of December before we close the books on this year.** So, I took a look at your supporter record, and I’m hoping you can help us out. Here’s what I found:
**Peter's 2022 Supporter Record**
**Email:** dpwillis67@gmail.com
**2022 Donor?:** No — not yet!**
****2022 Total:** $0
**Suggested donation:[$27](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/24241?refcode=em20221202-1913&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=1913%2E420207%2E0D63Li)**

_(FYI: If you donated recently, in the mail, or with a different email address, your record might not yet reflect your contribution. Thank you for your support!)_
Peter, this is our last chance to fundraise while it really counts. [**Your support truly makes a difference for us – now more than ever. Can you invest in Pramila for the first time today with a $27 donation?**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/24241?refcode=em20221202-1913&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=1913%2E420207%2E0D63Li)
**[Chip in $27 »](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/24241?refcode=em20221202-1913&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=22&akid=1913%2E420207%2E0D63Li)**
I’ve crunched the numbers and to be honest, it’s pretty tight. I know we *can* make up the difference, but with so little time left in the year, we’re going to need a groundswell of support.
We all know that nothing comes easy for progressives. We have to work harder and smarter when we’re up against millions of dollars from corporate and Super PACs trying to take down our movement. **But people power still wins -- our winning progressive candidates showed that this year. Our work is worth it, but it takes resources to keep winning.**
[**Peter, one last time: will you consider chipping in $27, or any amount to help us boost our numbers in December**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/24241?refcode=em20221202-1913&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=23&akid=1913%2E420207%2E0D63Li)
Thank you for supporting Pramila,
Lauren Lalonde, Campaign Manager
_Pramila for Congress_

**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20221202-1913-footer&t=24&refcode2=1913_420207_0D63Li&akid=1913%2E420207%2E0D63Li)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/pramila.jayapal) | [](https://twitter.com/pramilajayapal) | [](https://www.instagram.com/pramilajayapal/)
Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/did-your-info-change/?t=25&akid=1913%2E420207%2E0D63Li)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=26&akid=1913%2E420207%2E0D63Li)

Paid for by Pramila for Congress

body:

| | **President Obama is using his voice to amplify the critical importance of state legislatures:** [](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953xP1bRbO086_ZX-9NTtP6aHbAwXi1YWzwiVR22BOcczmQ2hPfF9jYPlUri51MG_xMMn9pbkTBGZYaB2Bbznlf10pJ8O7Zh275Tfhnevr_Izun4OZpOL8a5TvP8vMtrKs3UuS6lqrFKyUupN9bE7W6THrgjtPcPiL6xfL9fLkIyO2XtCFvWFbZfGHGhRPsPBhSLPvt1JWLMIoZmWrbYU9JSVzKlp4JTxCW5uHr5yIBtvNpjwjIm4ccplQZhGcpvcAPBc4ze58CKHmG1WGGy-VnBdKF0mRWxvoVnX8zDAhAWMWwu4zgEN132Dg46VtMl1UDinJ51NvtpODbs2fjpaXtvNe25SHg0PWMStvA3haDxXEMBq6HpAJpceqHkoo2nI-Xz7Yc0dP6zeahkYYQQNWoHc1IusgCOR0dCalvuMjwEvWw/3rn/r2EWs4eXRPCnGihqftwpxg/h0/80cnveUyta4xmyqJIrNY46ZEZHrzw-zVvii10SZrjsY) And no one would be better than President Obama – he served THREE terms in the Illinois State Senate! name: =?utf-8?Q?Courtney=20Geels?=
subject: Call to Action: Durham School Board Meeting - Who's with me?
body:
We need to stand up | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/81d1198350db/call-to-action-durham-school-board-meeting-whos-with-me?e=20ac8f046f)
---
| |
---
| |
Dear Peter,

November didn't turn out as we hoped, but that doesn't mean we stop pushing for family values in our community. I appreciate you, and I hope that we continue to stay involved in our communities even after the election.

As we move forward, I wanted to let you know about an upcoming meeting that I hope you will attend with me. The Durham School Board is having a meeting this coming Thursday, December 8th, at 5:30 p.m. at the Fuller Administration Building, Room #307, 511 Cleveland Street, Durham, North Carolina 27701. I am planning to arrive at 5:10pm and I hope you will join me.
---
|
---
---
|
The agenda for this meeting includes information and discussion on a draft of amended guidelines for gender supports is presented for discussion on updating and strengthening LGBTQ+ and Gender Supports. Most of this policy was pulled from Orange County's policy. The meeting allows for public comment with 3 minutes allotted for each person.

As left-leaning as Durham is, we have to protect our children and the teachers who disagree with these policies, but will have their hands tied if these policies are implemented. Some of the topics are around: children transitioning, pronouns, and bathroom policies.

The link to review the policy being reviewed is below. If you click "agenda," it is explained starting on page 51.
---
| [Click to see the Agenda](https://courtneygeelsforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0ca724a68d4210b231de53e89&id=e817658a5a&e=20ac8f046f "Click to see the Agenda")
---
| There is a group on social media that is pushing to add a task force to enforce the policies that would not allow anyone to be part of the task force with "alternative views." They also want teachers to be able to use the curriculum and books they choose without parental permission.

Our religious liberties are on the line, and it's not time to sit on the sidelines. Join me this coming Thursday evening to make your voice heard!
---
| |
---
---
| Courtney Geels
---
| | | | | | [](https://courtneygeelsforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0ca724a68d4210b231de53e89&id=89e85aeba6&e=20ac8f046f)

As left-leaning as Durham is, we have to protect our children and the teachers who disagree with these policies, but will have their hands tied if these policies are implemented. Some of the topics are around: children transitioning, pronouns, and bathroom policies. The link to review the policy being reviewed is below. If you click "agenda," it is explained starting on page 51. | "Click to see the Agenda" | There is a group on social media that is pushing to add a task force to enforce the policies that would not allow anyone to be part of the task force with "alternative views." They also want teachers to be able to use the curriculum and books they choose without parental permission. Our religious liberties are on the line, and it's not time to sit on the sidelines. Join me this coming Thursday evening to make your voice heard! | | | Courtney Geels | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Paid for byCourtney Geels for Congress Our mailing address is: Courtney Geels for Congress PO Box 995 Hillsborough, NC 27278 Want to change how you receive these emails? You can or . |

name: =?utf-8?Q?Illinois=20Review?=
subject: LATEST NEWS: Conservative update you can't miss
body:
Incredible theme emerging in private messages, social media posts and public comments on Illinois Review posts

View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/2b3e416e2bd1/latest-news-conservative-update-you-cant-miss?e=1cb5fdae9e)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=c24c4dac9c-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_02_09_50&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-c24c4dac9c-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

IL GOP Establishment to Grassroots: Only Rich, Donor Voices Matter (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2022/12/il-gop-establishment-to-grassroots-only-rich-donor-voices-matter.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=c24c4dac9c-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_02_09_50&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-c24c4dac9c-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

In private messages, social media posts and public comments on Illinois Review posts, a theme is emerging from the IL GOP establishment to the hardworking, grassroots conservative freedom fighters: only rich, donor voices matter.

It’s an elitist mentality – and for years, they’ve gotten away with it.

But that’s now changing.
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2022/12/il-gop-establishment-to-grassroots-only-rich-donor-voices-matter.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=c24c4dac9c-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_02_09_50&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-c24c4dac9c-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D
IL conservative grassroots activists join Tom DeVore for a press conference with Darren Bailey at The Drake Hotel in Oak Brook on Nov. 7, 2022. (Facebook / Tom DeVore)

============================================================

As embattled IL GOP chair Don Tracy struggles to maintain his control of the party, and with new revelations that his family-owned business, where he is an ** owner (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2022/12/il-gop-chair-don-tracys-family-owned-business-donated-to-biden-never-trumpers.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=c24c4dac9c-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_02_09_50&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-c24c4dac9c-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
, donated to Joe Biden in 2020 and ‘Never Trumpers’ in 2022, the small, but entrenched establishment is swinging back in a way that only out-of-touch elitists can.

Leslie Munger, a
** two-time failed candidate (https://ballotpedia.org/Leslie_Munger?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=c24c4dac9c-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_02_09_50&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-c24c4dac9c-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
who’s never won an election despite being appointed by Gov. Bruce Rauner to serve as Comptroller following the death of Judy Baar Topinka in 2014, commented on an IR article titled, “Embattled IL GOP Chair Don Tracy Becomes Distraction as Names Surface to Replace Him,” ** writing (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2022/11/embattled-il-gop-chair-don-tracy-becomes-distraction-as-names-surface-to-replace-him.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=c24c4dac9c-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_02_09_50&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-c24c4dac9c-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
:
“I’m struck by the number of people who have never run for office, never run a campaign, never canvassed thousands of doors, never raised millions of dollars to support Republican political efforts, and/or never contributed substantial funds to the ILGOP or a specific candidate are now the loudest voices complaining about the results of this election.”

Munger then finished her sentence with,
“I have done all of the above.”

In Thursday’s article titled, “IL GOP Chair Don Tracy’s Family-Owned Business Donated to Biden, ‘Never Trumpers,’” another elitist establishment-type named Darlene ** commented (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2022/12/il-gop-chair-don-tracys-family-owned-business-donated-to-biden-never-trumpers.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=c24c4dac9c-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_02_09_50&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-c24c4dac9c-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
,
“Why not point out his individual contributions to the GOP?...
Whoever wrote this article, I guarantee, has not donated as much as Mr Tracy has to GOP Causes...”

And former state central committee member Lynn O’Brien, who ** endorsed (https://capitolfax.com/2022/01/18/irvin-rolls-out-over-60-gop-endorsements/?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=c24c4dac9c-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_02_09_50&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-c24c4dac9c-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
the Ricahrd Irvin for Governor campaign, commented publicly on a ** Facebook post (https://www.facebook.com/RNCPORTER/posts/pfbid07A4q7hEh2dwN2yZcAoAGcD7EyCyiiLdRabGbcHQgiWHMroep1g9iNJYutEcUhJBQl?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=c24c4dac9c-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_02_09_50&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-c24c4dac9c-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
on Thursday, writing,
“We can't or shouldn't complain if we haven't given money, knock doors, attended events...”

Although this article has highlighted three different comments from the elitist, establishment-types, there are more. Much more.

Since the GOP establishment is so concerned with money, how can they explain $50 million donated to their candidate Richard Irvin, only to come in third place and lose to the conservative grassroots candidate by ** 42 points (https://elections.il.gov/electionoperations/ElectionVoteTotals.aspx?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=c24c4dac9c-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_02_09_50&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-c24c4dac9c-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
.

It wasn’t even close – and an embarrassing defeat for the GOP establishment.

As S. Murray, a conservative grassroots patriot
** commented (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2022/12/il-gop-chair-don-tracys-family-owned-business-donated-to-biden-never-trumpers.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=c24c4dac9c-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_02_09_50&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-c24c4dac9c-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
,
“The 3-T’s of being a good party member are TIME, TREASURE, and TALENT. Not everyone can give money, but just because they can give money does not make them somehow better than someone who gives the time (volunteering and doorknocking, especially) or the talent.”
To learn more about Illinois Review, visit ** www.illinoisreview.com (https://www.illinoisreview.com/?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=c24c4dac9c-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_02_09_50&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-c24c4dac9c-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
** Twitter (http://www.twitter.com/illinoisreview?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=c24c4dac9c-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_02_09_50&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-c24c4dac9c-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
** Facebook (http://www.facebook.com/illinois.review?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=c24c4dac9c-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_02_09_50&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-c24c4dac9c-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
** Website (https://www.illinoisreview.com/?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=c24c4dac9c-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_02_09_50&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-c24c4dac9c-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

name: GOP Alerts
subject: Time to make a move
disclaimer_text: Paid for by JD Vance for Senate Inc
body:
praying you open this

|
---
Friends,

We have some discouraging news to share with you.

We have asked for your help in the past, but we wouldn’t be coming to you again if this wasn’t **_urgent._**

name: Cliff Schecter, Blue Amp Action
subject: We beat so many of the radicalized figures that Trump elevated from the fringes of US politics in November’s election. Now we must do the same to Herschel Walker, so we can keep yet another Democrat, Raphael Warnock, in office, pushing for our goal of full equality for all.
body:
Trump’s dangerous friends
| | | | |
---
| | |
---
| | | _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T91a152c7-cb20-42e9-9daa-79bb999d663b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
|
---
| |
---
| | | | | Peter, | [**_$5. $10. $25. These are the kinds of donations that could make the difference between six more years of Senator Raphael Warnock ... or Senator-elect Herschel Walker. Here’s another reason why the December 6th runoff election matters to the whole country ..._**](https://default.salsalabs.org/Td29aba78-acf3-40e3-8a27-635b7a67d9ea/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) **_Donald Trump had a dinner at Mar-a-Lago with anti-Semite Kanye West and Holocaust denier Nick Fuentes. At first Trump tried to deny it, but it's been reported in multiple outlets right down to the specifics of the conversation._** **_Since then, Kanye praised Nazis in an interview with Alex Jones that, as one Twitter wag put it, made Alex Jones look like the reasonable person in the room. These are the kinds of friends Trump has._** _**Trump is also a close friend, political mentor and supporter of Herschel Walker's, who's made numerous despicably anti-Semitic remarks. Trump has repeatedly campaigned for Walker in Georgia’s critical Senate race.**_ _**Walker's election would further mainstream fringe figures who hold racist, misogynist, anti-gay and anti-Semitic views. It'****s becoming a dangerous threat to inspire more violence, of the kind we've seen most recently in Colorado Springs.**_ **_WALKER’S BRAND OF EXTREMISM PUTS ALL OF OUR FAMILIES IN DANGER._**
---

|
---
| | | | | | [**Donate: Help fight extremism in Georgia!**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T501c5be9-592b-46c6-8c16-80d8f4f1b1e4/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | **_We beat back many of the radicalized figures that Trump elevated from the fringes of US politics in November, candidates such as Kari Lake, Doug Mastriano and Tudor Dixon, to elect Democrats devoted to equality. Now we must do the same to Herschel Walker, so we can keep yet another Democrat, Raphael Warnock, in office, pushing for our goal of full equality for all._** [**_Will you join us in sending Senator Warnock $5, $10, $25 or whatever you can to keep Senator Warnock in office and stop hate from having yet another representative in the Senate?_**](https://default.salsalabs.org/Te741f072-a0ca-4eb9-a1f1-0e564d9dbf19/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) **Thank you for caring about ALL Americans.**
---
Cliff

| | | About BlueAmp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. clean_body: The entire political world has descended on Georgia. | Friend, Here we go again. The outcome of the Senate runoff in Georgia will – just like last election cycle – have critical national implications. November 8th was not the 'Red Wave' many had predicted. However, if you still want to turn this country around, we've got one more chance. The Libertarian candidate in our race will not proceed to the December 6th runoff, so the 80,000+ votes he received on November 8th are up for grabs. This number is important because Senator Warnock and I are separated by just a little more than 40,000 votes. The entire political world has descended on Georgia. Democrats are spending hundreds of millions to take me down, which could make this the most expensive Senate race in history. We need your help RIGHT NOW to fund our 'Get-Out-The-Vote' advertising. Thank you, Herschel Walker | | PAID FOR BY TEAM HERSCHEL, INC.

Peter, I wrote you earlier to invite you tonight's virtual For the Future Rally with Jasmine and Maxwell. They are going to be joining us before they fly back home from Washington D.C., where they have been all week during the second session of Freshman Congressional Orientation -- and they have some BIG NEWS TO SHARE with us all! The next election cycle will really test the ability of every progressive champion we have elected, to be effective in Congress. Progressive numbers are growing and we need strong leadership to navigate a GOP-controlled House. If you already RSVP'd make sure to share with your friends! In Solidarity, Roza Calderon

\- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - \- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - Peter, This election cycle was tough and Future Generations took on some of the biggest challenges in battleground states like Pennsylvania, Texas, and Florida. We had many incredible wins because of your continued support. **Tonight we celebrate two historic wins and we will get to hear from Congresswoman-Elect Jasmine Crockett and Congressman-Elect Maxwell Alejandro Frost!** I get chills writing this thinking about the journey that got us to this day. Jasmine Crockett was our first endorsement in Texas in 2020, when she first ran for State House in Texas against an establishment Democrat. With just $400 _(from small dollar donations)_ we mobilized an army of volunteers and we won the runoff by just 91 votes! Today Jasmine is wrapping up her second week of Freshman Orientation for U.S. Congress with Greg Casar, Summer Lee, and Maxwell Alejandro Frost. It was just over a year and a half that we met Maxwell -- we were his first endorsement because we could see the passion he possessed and we quickly organized to help the 1st Gen-Z member of Congress get elected! **[Will you join us tonight to celebrate TWO HISTORIC WINS?](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDKiO1DRELVicSxRd4Mydk5lv_brem0pQS6_iSKjRa1jUPOklQQ4_Grm4_Mn75fm-D5fPyD-m0E-B8AXa6eoP6Ha106jNYwYT8oBdrKSjBxGJiP8_5tTQQNw3PNvYbzlHf12aWqE8rT0QKxpfZiZAEN8ZkF7JHrQiNQYaYCjk1HPmIuP-4TOaIxJPTVk9XHvSPwdRBDLntadlwK5uGRsteUSTH8lF58yX_aAWPbn2nLVe_r_BYvR_w_pr74s4ze1VreZzOsQVzvFmX3gw4ta0kFjp07P4uFhHTIcGtzPN6j0bDoU-K6bLnjtsbVKOCmoZ3PfNErSsXm7gxq6Nw_OaZdfweqieYvMdYi9QlvSAAtdM/3rn/HBn3j6SQTxiyAyib15qxZA/h2/7BhnQhUMv4dNt6AqvkkYFYy273r4A4bZkz9ixVmQxyQ)**

Tonight's event will be hosted by two incredible Gen-Z activists who like Maxwell Frost are already veteran organizers. Anish Mohanty is a digital organizer for progressive candidates at the federal, state, and local level across California and a Commissioner for Union City. Anish has worked with Future Generations as our Creative Director on campaigns like Ed Markey and Andy Levin. Chitra Calderon is an environmental activist and has been a fellow with Future Generations on multiple campaigns like Cori Bush, Jasmine Crockett, Andy Levin, and Greg Casar. I am moved by Gen-Z’s ability to reach and mobilize voters, and for stopping the so-called "Red Wave." This year alone we won big in Pennsylvania with John Fetterman and Summer Lee, Maxwell Frost in Florida, Jasmine Crockett and Greg Casar in Texas, Aisha Wahab in California, and we are one week away from electing Zo Qadri to Austin City Council! We must continue doing our part to support young organizers who are making the difference and electing a diverse group of young candidates! clean_body: We are just an hour away from tonight's event, did you RSVP yet? | | Peter, I wrote you earlier to invite you tonight's virtual For the Future Rally with Jasmine and Maxwell. They are going to be joining us before they fly back home from Washington D.C., where they have been all week during the second session of Freshman Congressional Orientation -- and they have some BIG NEWS TO SHARE with us all! The next election cycle will really test the ability of every progressive champion we have elected, to be effective in Congress. Progressive numbers are growing and we need strong leadership to navigate a GOP-controlled House. If you already RSVP'd make sure to share with your friends! In Solidarity, Roza Calderon \- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - \- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - Peter, This election cycle was tough and Future Generations took on some of the biggest challenges in battleground states like Pennsylvania, Texas, and Florida. We had many incredible wins because of your continued support. Tonight we celebrate two historic wins and we will get to hear from Congresswoman-Elect Jasmine Crockett and Congressman-Elect Maxwell Alejandro Frost! I get chills writing this thinking about the journey that got us to this day. Jasmine Crockett was our first endorsement in Texas in 2020, when she first ran for State House in Texas against an establishment Democrat. With just $400 (from small dollar donations) we mobilized an army of volunteers and we won the runoff by just 91 votes! Today Jasmine is wrapping up her second week of Freshman Orientation for U.S. Congress with Greg Casar, Summer Lee, and Maxwell Alejandro Frost. It was just over a year and a half that we met Maxwell -- we were his first endorsement because we could see the passion he possessed and we quickly organized to help the 1st Gen-Z member of Congress get elected! | | | Tonight's event will be hosted by two incredible Gen-Z activists who like Maxwell Frost are already veteran organizers. Anish Mohanty is a digital organizer for progressive candidates at the federal, state, and local level across California and a Commissioner for Union City. Anish has worked with Future Generations as our Creative Director on campaigns like Ed Markey and Andy Levin. Chitra Calderon is an environmental activist and has been a fellow with Future Generations on multiple campaigns like Cori Bush, Jasmine Crockett, Andy Levin, and Greg Casar. I am moved by Gen-Z’s ability to reach and mobilize voters, and for stopping the so-called "Red Wave." This year alone we won big in Pennsylvania with John Fetterman and Summer Lee, Maxwell Frost in Florida, Jasmine Crockett and Greg Casar in Texas, Aisha Wahab in California, and we are one week away from electing Zo Qadri to Austin City Council! We must continue doing our part to support young organizers who are making the difference and electing a diverse group of young candidates! If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately. We drive ourselves against the odds to help our candidates fight in tough races across the country because we know that if people are given a real choice in who represents them, they will choose someone who will fight to secure their future today and for future generations. If I don't see you at tonight's event, I hope you will donate or sign up to volunteer! In solidarity, Roza Calderon Executive Director, Future Generations | | Future Generations is a youth-founded, youth-led movement guided by the Seventh Generations' philosophy of building a sustainable world for the next seven generations. We support, recruit, train, and endorse candidates who are committed to securing a just society and livable planet. In 2020 we helped re-elect GND champion Sen. Ed Markey and flipped crucial down-ballot seats across Texas. In 2022, we helped elect Jasmine Crockett (TX-30), Greg Casar (TX-35), and Maxwell Frost (FL-10), the first Gen-Z member in Congress. | | Connect with us by clicking on the links below: | | | Paid for by Future Generations PAC. ---|--- Future Generations PAC P.O.
